use serde::Deserialize;

#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]
struct Credentials {
    username: String,
    #[serde(deserialize_with = "validate_password")]
    password: String,
    #[serde(deserialize_with = "validate_token")]
    api_token: String,
}

fn validate_password<'de, D: serde::Deserializer<'de>>(d: D) -> Result<String, D::Error> {
    let s = String::deserialize(d)?;
    if s.len() < 8 {
        return Err(serde::de::Error::custom("password too short"));
    }
    Ok(s)
}

fn validate_token<'de, D: serde::Deserializer<'de>>(d: D) -> Result<String, D::Error> {
    let s = String::deserialize(d)?;
    if s.is_empty() {
        return Err(serde::de::Error::custom("token cannot be empty"));
    }
    Ok(s)
}

#[derive(Debug)]
struct SecretKey(String);

impl<'de> Deserialize<'de> for SecretKey {
    fn deserialize<D: serde::Deserializer<'de>>(d: D) -> Result<Self, D::Error> {
        let raw = String::deserialize(d)?;
        if raw.len() < 32 {
            return Err(serde::de::Error::custom("key too short"));
        }
        Ok(SecretKey(raw))
    }
}
